name: South Wonston
overview: South Wonston is a village on the downland north of Winchester in Hampshire, with a parish population estimated at around 2,870 (2017). The centre lies along Downs Road, where the Post Office and stores, primary school, St Margaret's Church, village hall and social club are clustered. It is largely a 20th-century village of houses and bungalows, popular with Winchester commuters.
character: A friendly, self-contained downland commuter village with good community facilities, though without a traditional pub (a members' social club fills that role).
amenities: Village shop and Post Office (Downs Road)
Branch surgery (of Gratton Surgery, Sutton Scotney)
Recreation ground with two football pitches, tennis court, pavilion, bike track, skate park, basketball court and play park

prices.summary: Predominantly detached houses and bungalows built largely in the mid-20th century; most common council tax bands are E and F. Popular with Winchester commuters. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Walks on the surrounding Hampshire Downs
Recreation ground sports and skate park
Community events at the village hall and social club
placesToEat: Eateries in nearby Winchester and Sutton Scotney
pros: Strong community facilities including an excellent recreation ground
Village shop, Post Office, school and surgery on the doorstep
Quick commute to Winchester and good A34/M3 access
cons: No traditional pub (members' social club only)
No railway station; rely on Winchester
Largely modern housing stock with less period character
whoItSuits: Families and commuters wanting a well-equipped downland village close to Winchester with strong sports and community amenities.
primarySupermarket: Village shop and Post Office; supermarkets in Winchester

sources.label: Check My Postcode - SO21 3EH (council tax, prices)
